text.title: Design Tokens Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Tokens Fundamentals. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Tokens are organised in exactly three tiers: global (raw primitives) → semantic (intent aliases) → component (scoped overrides). Two-tier or four-tier setups are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without the hierarchy, semantic tokens hold raw values and component tokens reference semantics directly — both defeat the 'update once, apply everywhere' guarantee.

rule.statement: Semantic tokens MUST alias a global token via {global.token.path} reference syntax; raw values in the semantic tier are CI-rejected.
rule.rationale: A semantic token holding a raw hex must be updated in two places when the brand color changes; CI gate is the only reliable enforcement.

rule.statement: Semantic tokens are named by intent (color.feedback.error, spacing.md) not by value (color.red, spacing.16); value-based names in the semantic tier are rejected.
rule.rationale: Value-based names ossify; intent-based names survive rebrands and theme changes.

rule.statement: Either Figma Variables OR a tokens JSON in the repo — never both as independent sources. The chosen source is named in an ADR.
rule.rationale: Without a single source, Figma and code diverge within weeks; the token-based theming becomes unreliable.

rule.statement: Breaking token renames bump the major version and ship release notes; additions are minor; value tweaks are patch.
rule.rationale: Without versioning discipline, downstream packages silently break on rename.

rule.statement: CI pipeline runs Style Dictionary build + lint; any semantic token with a raw value fails the build.
rule.rationale: Manual review does not scale; automated enforcement is the only reliable governance mechanism.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-tokens-fundamentals.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
